steps.title: Identify the barrier to knowing results
steps.content: Text 2 grants that studies report positive results but then pivots: methodological shortcomings prevent firm conclusions. The Hansemark example drives this—a 19-student sample makes it “difficult to say whether the training actually had an effect.” This uncertainty directly answers the question “But what results do such programs produce?”

steps.title: Match the critique to the choices
steps.content: Choice B correctly states that results are unknown because studies lack adequate design for definitive conclusions. Choices A, C, and D either overstate the evidence, miss the target of the critique, or introduce claims Text 2 doesn't make. That matches choice B — B is correct.
